categories.name: أَعْدَادٌ كَمِّيَّةٌ مِنْ أَرْبَعَةٍ إِلَى سَبْعَةٍ
categories.nameEn: Cardinal numbers from four to seven

items.verse: لَّوْلَا جَآءُو عَلَيْهِ بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَدَآءَ ۚ فَإِذْ لَمْ يَأْتُوا۟ بِٱلشُّهَدَآءِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ هُمُ ٱلْكَـٰذِبُونَ
items.verseEn: Why did they [who slandered] not produce for it four witnesses? And when they do not produce the witnesses, then it is they, in the sight of Allāh, who are the liars.

items.verse: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ مَا يَكُونُ مِن نَّجْوَىٰ ثَلَـٰثَةٍ إِلَّا هُوَ رَابِعُهُمْ وَلَا خَمْسَةٍ إِلَّا هُوَ سَادِسُهُمْ وَلَآ أَدْنَىٰ مِن ذَٰلِكَ وَلَآ أَكْثَرَ إِلَّا هُوَ مَعَهُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كَانُوا۟ ۖ ثُمَّ يُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا عَمِلُوا۟ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
items.verseEn: Have you not considered that Allāh knows what is in the heavens and what is on the earth? There are not three in a private conversation but that He is the fourth of them, nor are there five but that He is the sixth of them - and no less than that and no more except that He is with them [in knowledge] wherever they are. Then He will inform them of what they did, on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed Allāh is, of all things, Knowing.

items.verse: هُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍۢ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ ۚ يَعْلَمُ مَا يَلِجُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا وَمَا يَنزِلُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَا ۖ وَهُوَ مَعَكُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌۭ
items.verseEn: It is He who created the heavens and earth in six days and then established Himself above the Throne. He knows what penetrates into the earth and what emerges from it and what descends from the heaven and what ascends therein; and He is with you wherever you are. And Allāh, of what you do, is Seeing.

items.verse: هُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ لَكُم مَّا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًۭا ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰٓ إِلَى ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَسَوَّىٰهُنَّ سَبْعَ سَمَـٰوَٰتٍۢ ۚ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: It is He who created for you all of that which is on the earth. Then He directed Himself to the heaven, [His being above all creation], and made them seven heavens, and He is Knowing of all things.

categories.name: آيَاتٌ وَبَرَاهِينُ
categories.nameEn: Terms for divine signs, proofs, and authority

items.verse: وَإِذَآ أَذَقْنَا ٱلنَّاسَ رَحْمَةًۭ مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ضَرَّآءَ مَسَّتْهُمْ إِذَا لَهُم مَّكْرٌۭ فِىٓ ءَايَاتِنَا ۚ قُلِ ٱللَّهُ أَسْرَعُ مَكْرًا ۚ إِنَّ رُسُلَنَا يَكْتُبُونَ مَا تَمْكُرُونَ
items.verseEn: And when We give the people a taste of mercy after adversity has touched them, at once they conspire against Our verses. Say, "Allāh is swifter in strategy." Indeed, Our messengers [i.e., angels] record that which you conspire.

items.verse: ٱسْلُكْ يَدَكَ فِى جَيْبِكَ تَخْرُجْ بَيْضَآءَ مِنْ غَيْرِ سُوٓءٍۢ وَٱضْمُمْ إِلَيْكَ جَنَاحَكَ مِنَ ٱلرَّهْبِ ۖ فَذَٰنِكَ بُرْهَـٰنَانِ مِن رَّبِّكَ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًۭا فَـٰسِقِينَ
items.verseEn: Insert your hand into the opening of your garment; it will come out white, without disease. And draw in your arm close to you [as prevention] from fear, for those are two proofs from your Lord to Pharaoh and his establishment. Indeed, they have been a people defiantly disobedient."

items.verse: قَالَتْ لَهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ إِن نَّحْنُ إِلَّا بَشَرٌۭ مِّثْلُكُمْ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَمُنُّ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ لَنَآ أَن نَّأْتِيَكُم بِسُلْطَـٰنٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ
items.verseEn: Their messengers said to them, "We are only men like you, but Allāh confers favor upon whom He wills of His servants. It has never been for us to bring you evidence except by permission of Allāh. And upon Allāh let the believers rely.

items.verse: ۞ وَيَسْتَنۢبِـُٔونَكَ أَحَقٌّ هُوَ ۖ قُلْ إِى وَرَبِّىٓ إِنَّهُۥ لَحَقٌّۭ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ
items.verseEn: And they ask information of you, [O Muḥammad], "Is it true?" Say, "Yes, by my Lord. Indeed, it is truth; and you will not cause failure [to Allāh]."

categories.name: أَوْصَافٌ قُرْآنِيَّةٌ لِلْمَاءِ أَوْ مَصْدَرِهِ
categories.nameEn: Specific Quranic descriptors for water or its source

items.verse: لَوْ نَشَآءُ جَعَلْنَـٰهُ أُجَاجًۭا فَلَوْلَا تَشْكُرُونَ
items.verseEn: If We willed, We could make it bitter, so why are you not grateful?

items.verse: أَوْ يُصْبِحَ مَآؤُهَا غَوْرًۭا فَلَن تَسْتَطِيعَ لَهُۥ طَلَبًۭا
items.verseEn: Or its water will become sunken [into the earth], so you would never be able to seek it."

items.verse: ءَأَنتُمْ أَنزَلْتُمُوهُ مِنَ ٱلْمُزْنِ أَمْ نَحْنُ ٱلْمُنزِلُونَ
items.verseEn: Is it you who brought it down from the clouds, or is it We who bring it down?
And sent down, from the rain clouds, pouring water.

categories.name: أَلْفَاظٌ لِأَنْوَاعٍ مِنَ الْجَمَاعَاتِ
categories.nameEn: Words for different types of groups or companies

items.verseEn: A [large] company of the former peoples

items.verse: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰشُعَيْبُ مَا نَفْقَهُ كَثِيرًۭا مِّمَّا تَقُولُ وَإِنَّا لَنَرَىٰكَ فِينَا ضَعِيفًۭا ۖ وَلَوْلَا رَهْطُكَ لَرَجَمْنَـٰكَ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيْنَا بِعَزِيزٍۢ
items.verseEn: They said, "O Shuʿayb, we do not understand much of what you say, and indeed, we consider you among us as weak. And if not for your family, we would have stoned you [to death]; and you are not to us one respected."

items.verse: قُلْ أُوحِىَ إِلَىَّ أَنَّهُ ٱسْتَمَعَ نَفَرٌۭ مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ فَقَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا سَمِعْنَا قُرْءَانًا عَجَبًۭا
items.verseEn: Say, [O Muḥammad], "It has been revealed to me that a group of the jinn listened and said, 'Indeed, we have heard an amazing Qur’ān [i.e., recitation].

items.verse: يَـٰبَنِىٓ ءَادَمَ لَا يَفْتِنَنَّكُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ كَمَآ أَخْرَجَ أَبَوَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ يَنزِعُ عَنْهُمَا لِبَاسَهُمَا لِيُرِيَهُمَا سَوْءَٰتِهِمَآ ۗ إِنَّهُۥ يَرَىٰكُمْ هُوَ وَقَبِيلُهُۥ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا تَرَوْنَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينَ أَوْلِيَآءَ لِلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
items.verseEn: O children of Adam, let not Satan tempt you as he removed your parents from Paradise, stripping them of their clothing to show them their private parts. Indeed, he sees you, he and his tribe, from where you do not see them. Indeed, We have made the devils allies to those who do not believe.

harf.hint: The ultimate reward, a garden of eternal bliss.
harf.arabicVerse: ۞ وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ سُعِدُوا۟ فَفِى ٱلْجَنَّةِ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا مَا دَامَتِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ رَبُّكَ ۖ عَطَآءً غَيْرَ مَجْذُوذٍۢ
harf.verse: 11:108 — And as for those who were [destined to be] prosperous, they will be in Paradise, abiding therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except what your Lord should will - a bestowal uninterrupted.

deduction.title: The Tale of the Sealed Well
deduction.intro: A community received guidance but chose arrogance. Their story, etched in the annals of time, serves as a stark reminder of the consequences of rejecting truth. They silenced the voice of their warner, and in doing so, sealed their own fate. Can you uncover the identity of this lost community and the event that led to their ruin?
clues: We were a community among many who received a messenger to guide us.
Like the people of 'Ad and Thamud, we are mentioned in the Quran as a people who denied the truth.
Our story is a cautionary tale, a lesson for generations to come about the fate of those who disbelieve.
We lived by a source of water, a feature so central to our identity that it became part of our name.
We rejected our prophet, and for our disbelief, we faced a terrible punishment from Allah.
Our name, 'Ashab ar-Rass', literally translates to the 'Companions of the Well', forever linking our identity to our transgression.

trial.label: What was our primary sin?
options: Building a lofty tower to challenge God
Arrogantly boasting about a magnificent garden

outcome.label: What was our fate?
options: Guided to the straight path and forgiven
Saved by a believing insider from among them

deduction.arabic: وَعَادًۭا وَثَمُودَا۟ وَأَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلرَّسِّ وَقُرُونًۢا بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ كَثِيرًۭا
deduction.verse: And [We destroyed] ʿAad and Thamūd and the companions of the well and many generations between them. (25:38)

translations: He guides his heart And Allah
scramble.hint: This verse speaks about finding guidance and peace through faith when facing life's difficulties.
scramble.arabic: مَآ أَصَابَ مِن مُّصِيبَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَمَن يُؤْمِنۢ بِٱللَّهِ يَهْدِ قَلْبَهُۥ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌۭ
scramble.verseEn: No disaster strikes except by permission of Allāh. And whoever believes in Allāh - He will guide his heart. And Allāh is Knowing of all things.

verse.arabic_text: كِتَـٰبٌ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ إِلَيْكَ مُبَـٰرَكٌ لِّيَدَّبَّرُوٓا۟ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ وَلِيَتَذَكَّرَ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ
verse.translation: [This is] a blessed Book which We have revealed to you, that they might reflect upon its verses and that those of understanding would be reminded.

theme_question.correct: The primary purpose of the Quran's revelation is for deep reflection and remembrance.
options: The primary purpose of the Quran's revelation is for deep reflection and remembrance.
The Quran's main function is to be recited for blessings without understanding.
The Quran was revealed only as a historical record of past nations.
The Book was sent down exclusively for the people of Makkah.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse is a powerful declaration about the very nature and purpose of the Quran. It comes within Surah Sad, which discusses themes of prophethood, patience, and accountability. After recounting stories of past prophets like David and Solomon to console Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ), Allah emphasizes that this revealed Book is not mere storytelling but a blessed source of guidance meant for profound contemplation.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The theme is "Tadabbur al-Quran"—reflecting upon the Quran. The verse highlights that the Quran's blessings are unlocked not just through ritual recitation, but through deep, thoughtful engagement with its meanings. It invites "Ulul-Albab" (people of understanding) to ponder its signs, derive lessons, and remember Allah. This active reflection is what transforms the reader and brings the guidance of the scripture to life.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Sad (ص) is a Makkan surah named after the Arabic letter with which it begins. It addresses the disbelief of the Quraysh, comforting the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) by narrating the trials and triumphs of previous prophets, particularly Dawud (David) and Sulayman (Solomon), and the patience of Ayyub (Job). The surah powerfully contrasts the fate of the righteous in Paradise with the despair of the disbelievers in Hell, and concludes by reaffirming the core message of Tawhid (the Oneness of Allah).
